title: Contract No. 32000118 Amendment: NEXGEN Asset and Work Order Management Software Maintenance

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract No.
32000118 with NEXGEN Asset Management to provide additional funding for software
maintenance and professional services, increasing the amount by $1,123,457, for a total
not-to-exceed amount of $2,830,366 through June 30, 2029.
sections.current_situation:
The departments of Public Works and Parks, Recreation and Waterfront use NEXGEN’s
Asset and Work Order management system to perform all maintenance and asset
management for the City. Implemented in 2021, NEXGEN streamlines the work order
process, makes services more accessible, and replaces paper-based processes with
workflow automation and mobile technology. City staff use custom reports to gain in-depth
insights for informed decision making and leverage City specific end user guides to provide
an improved user experience, efficient onboarding, and technical accuracy. The City
continues to partner with the vendor to further enhance the system with high-quality
guidelines and standards. The City seeks additional funding to pay for year 4 through year
8 software maintenance.
NEXGEN advances the City’s strategic goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained
infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
The City’s previous work order and maintenance management systems (FUND$, Accela,
and Verint) were antiquated and inadequate to perform proper maintenance of City assets
Page 3
Page 4 of 6
Public
Contract No. 32000118 Amendment: NEXGEN CMMS/EAM CONSENT CALENDAR
March 25, 2025
and retrieve information needed to comply with mandated reporting requirements and
internal performance measures. These systems were not well integrated, lacked digital field
communications, and could not provide effective fiscal management and data analytics
typically offered by a modern Computerized Maintenance Management System/Enterprise
Asset Management (CMMS/EAM) system. The situation led to inefficiencies in business
workflow, delayed community response times, and limited reporting and analysis required
to manage City assets effectively.
On October 23, 2018, the City issued Request for Proposals (RFP) #19-11259-C for an
improved CMMS/EAM System based upon business analysis completed by the
Departments of Parks, Recreation and Waterfront, Public Works and Information
Technology in partnership with ThirdWave Corporation.
The City received seven (7) responses to the CMMS/EAM System RFP by the December
18, 2018 deadline. A committee of 12+ staff from the Parks, Recreation & Waterfront, Public
Works, and Information Technology departments conducted a comprehensive evaluation
process that included proposal reviews, vendor demonstrations, and reference checks. Staff
selected NEXGEN Asset Management as presenting the most comprehensive, user-
responsive, and effective solution for the City’s current and future work order and asset
management needs.
In 2019, with Council approval, the City entered into a contract agreement (No. 32000118)
with NEXGEN Asset Management to provide the new work order and asset management
software. In November 2021, the software went live.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
An improved system, with greater efficiency, lessens the number of trips field emp
sections.rationale:
Software maintenance is required. Further, NexGen has provided prompt resolution for
issues and requests and will continue to provide ongoing support and maintenance for the
system.
sections.alternatives:
None. City staff do not recommend using this enterprise software without software support
and maintenance.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6541

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 5
Page 6 of 6
Public
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32000118 AMENDMENT: NEXGEN ASSET AND WORK ORDER
MANAGEMENT SOFTWARE MAINTENANCE
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley has identified the need to improve the efficiency of its
asset management and work order processes; and
WHEREAS, on October 23, 2018, the City of Berkeley issued Request for Proposal No.
19-11259-C for an improved computerized maintenance management system &
enterprise asset management system and received seven bids; and
WHEREAS, in response to the City’s Request for Proposal, NEXGEN Asset Management
responded with a proposal that was rated as the highest response submitted by
competing vendors; and
WHEREAS, on June 11, 2019, by Resolution No. 68,948 - N.S., the Council approved a
contract (No. 32000118) with NEXGEN Asset Management for software hosting,
implementation, maintenance and related services, for an amount not to exceed
$1,017,509; and
WHEREAS, on January 31, 2023, by Resolution No. 70,677 - N.S., the Council approved
a contract amendment (No. 32000118) with NEXGEN Asset Management for software
maintenance and professional services related, for an amount not to exceed $1,706,909;
and
WHEREAS, funding for this recommendation will be provided by the FY 2025, 2026,
2027, 2028, and 2029 Public Works, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront Software
Maintenance and Parks Tax funds, and spending for this contract and related
amendments in future fiscal years is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed city-
wide budget and annual appropriation ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 32000118 with NEXGEN Asset
Management for additional funding, increasing the amount by $1,123,457, for a total not-
to-exceed amount of $2,830,366 for software maintenance through June 30, 2029.
